title: Significance of Cultivating Emotional Intelligence | Sri Satya Gaur Chandra Dasa | SB 3.22.24-25
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: entitled, The Marriage of Kardama Muni and Devahuti.
Verse number 24, 25. 24, I will read myself.
Translation, Thus relieved of his responsibility by handing over his daughter to a suitable man,
Swayambhuva Manu, his mind agitated by feeling of separation,
embraced his affectionate daughter with both his arms,
purport by his divine recessive attidanda swamishila brahupa, Jai Brahma.
A father always remains in anxiety until he can hand over his grown-up daughter to a suitable boy.
A father and mother's responsibility for children continue until they marry them to suitable spouses.
When the father is able to perform that duty, he is relieved of his responsibility.
Translation and purport by his divine recessive attidanda swamishila brahupa, Jai Brahma.
Translation and purport by his divine recessive attidanda swamishila brahupa, Jai Brahma.
The emperor was unable to bear the separation of his daughter.
Therefore, tears poured from his eyes again and again, drenching his daughter's head as he cried,
My dear mother, my dear daughter.
Purport by his divine recessive attidanda swamishila brahupa, Jai Brahma.
A father sometimes addresses his daughter in affection as mother,
The feeling of separation utters because until the daughter is married,
she remains the daughter of the father.
But after her marriage, she is no longer claimed as a daughter in the family.
She must go to the husband's house.
For after marriage, she becomes the property of the husband.
According to Manu Samhita, a woman is never independent.
She must remain the property of the father while she is not married,
and she must remain the property of the husband
until she is elderly and has grown up children of her own.
In old age, when the husband has taken sannyas and left home,
she remains the property of the sons.
A woman is always dependent either upon the father, husband, or elderly sons.
That will be exhibited in the life of Devahuti.
for her, to the husband, Tridamamuni.
And in the same way, Tridamamuni also left home,
giving the responsibility to his son, Trapiladev.
This narration will describe these events one after another.
So as soon as the marriage got over,
was given to a great sage by name Khardhamamuni.
immediately he is relieved from his responsibility.
So the Vedic culture is about responsibilities,
and it is not about rights.
Fighting for rights is a modern phenomena,
but Vedic culture is full of responsibilities,
he is so affectionate towards the daughter,
and as soon as she is married,
he felt relieved of the responsibility.
now my daughter is going to go away,
feelings of separation appeared in his heart.
that the children should settle in life,
and as soon as they marry them,
they feel a certain relief of the responsibility.
the separation from the daughter was so intense,
affectionate dealings used to be there,
the children are very affectionate to,
and then the parents are very,
even the smallest of the sensitivities,
that he is getting separated from the daughter,
and so there is to treat her,
he used to do his own,
he sent a big message to us,
